observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 on the proper use of government vehicles and instruct the Supply Officer to properly marked with the words “For Official Use Only” (3 inches) in the agency’s owned vehicles, under which should be written the name of the Municipality of Tadian, Mountain Province, to prevent unauthorized use or improper use of the vehicles.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the contractors concerned to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures to address the noted defects to prevent further deterioration and early destruction of the project; and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to compute and collect/deduct the corresponding liquidated damages from any money due to the contractors pursuant to the provisions of Item 8, Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the Local Chief Executive as well as the members of the Sanggunian Bayan ensure that any realignment from the savings of the  approved  budget  shall  be  made  thru an appropriation ordinance covering supplemental budget and not by way of SB resolution in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and the Budget Operations Manual.

We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the following, in order to properly account and control gasoline/fuel, oil and lubricants consumption, and use of government vehicles:
Monthly Report of Official Travel, together with the original copy of the Driver’s Trip Ticket, summarizing in chronological order the driver’s trip for the month, duly certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer; and
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption showing among others, the odometer readings, total distance traveled, total fuel used, and normal fuel consumption for the vehicle.

We have recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are serially numbered, properly accomplished, and include the required information as provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 77-61.
We have recommended further and Management agreed to instruct the officials’ in-charge of the motor vehicles to check all defective fuel gauge, odometer and speedometer of all vehicles that need to be repaired; otherwise, replace those defective ones in order to measure the gasoline usage and the distance travelled of the vehicles; and to avoid wasteful spending of government funds.

We have recommended and Management agreed to make representation with the PCSO and DENR managements for the cost of the above-mentioned donated vehicles; and if not available, determine the fair market value of the donated equipment, and furnish the Municipal Accountant copies of the transfer documents as basis for recording in the books of the Municipality.
We have recommended further and Management agreed to henceforth, strictly comply with the provision of Section 63 of P.D. No. 1445, on all moneys and property officially received must be accounted for as government funds and property and shall be taken up in the books of the agency.

We have recommended and Management agreed to maintain a special account in the General Fund for the 20% Municipal Development Fund through the use of a complete subsidiary ledger, and to prepare financial statements at the end of the year which will form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the pertinent provisions on the Leave of Absence in accordance with the Omnibus Rules on Leave and Republic Act No. 7160.
We have recommended further and Management further agreed to ensure that local elective officials and employees who are absent during their sessions shall file their respective leaves of absence to be entitled to receive their salary, otherwise, payment made to them shall be disallowed in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure that the BAC, its Secretariat and TWG members attend procurement trainings or capacity development programs authorized or accredited by the GPPB through its Technical Support Office pursuant to the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184, updated as of March 31, 2021.

We have recommended and Management agreed to fast track the implementation of the LDRRM infrastructure projects and procurement of equipment to achieve the purpose for which the fund was appropriated and in order for the stakeholders to enjoy the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

In order to implement a systematic and effective review process with a view of generating timely and relevant audit results, we have recommended and Management agreed to:

 Immediately submit copy of the contract including all its supporting documents for review and evaluation; and

Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 in the submission of contracts including supporting documents to avoid administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 1 | Management submitted copy of the contract including all its supporting documents.

Management submitted copy of the contract.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office make sure that all disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation using as guide the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001, prior to approval.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 2 | For CY 2021, disbursement vouchers for infrastructure projects were supported with complete supporting documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification; and henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements for emergency purchase under R.A. No. 11469 are complied with before certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 3 | Management submitted the lacking 16 Mayor’s Permit, ITRs and OSS.

Also, Management made constant reminders to the suppliers for them to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the BAC undertake the procurement of goods under emergency purchases (Negotiated Procurement) following the guidelines under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 4 | For CY 2021, the BAC undertook procurement of goods under emergency purchases following the implementing guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure that all the relevant and required information in the Purchase Order are properly accomplished to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods/items in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 5 | For CY 2021, all the relevant and required information in the POs were properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the abovementioned documents be immediately submitted   to the audit team to ensure the validity and propriety of the amount claimed, and to avoid disallowance.

Henceforth, Management ensure that all claims against government funds must be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 6 | Management submitted all the lacking documentary requirements.



Management ensured that all claims against government funds were supported with completed documentation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure the proper utilization of funds in accordance with its implementing guidelines to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 7 | Management made the necessary adjustment. Also, for CY 2021, funds were properly utilized in accordance with its implementing guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, require its suppliers to post a warranty security by either retention money equivalent to at least 1 percent but not to exceed 5 percent of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent but not to exceed five percent of the total contract price, to make sure that manufacturing defects shall be corrected by the suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 8 | For CY 2021, Management required the suppliers to post the warranty security.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, utilize the QRF only after issuance of a Local Sanggunian Resolution to support such release including an itemized utilization thereof pursuant to Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We  have  recommended further and Management agreed that for succeeding transactions, secure authorization from the Sanggunian before any disbursements from a lump-sum appropriation is made pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2010-014.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 9 | For CY 2021, QRF were only utilized after the issuance of SB Resolution Nos. 24-2021 dated Jan. 6,2021, 140-2021 dated July 26,2021 and 199-2021 dated No. 16,2021 including LDRRMFIP pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 and COA Circular No. 2012-002

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure the preparation of the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submit the same within ten  working days after the end of each quarter to the NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnished the COA Auditors in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 10 | Management submitted the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances within the required period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to make representation with the Office of the Civil Defense and the Department of Health for the cost/value of the donated items.  And if not available, determine the fair market value of the donated equipment, and furnish the Municipal Accountant copies of the transfer documents as basis for recording in the books of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 11 | Management determined the cost of the donated equipment and furnished the Municipal Accountant of the transfer documents. The Municipal Accountant recorded the said equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management adjust the affected accounts. 

We  have recommended further and Management agreed to henceforth, recognize the collection from the sale of bid documents and the payment of honorarium of BAC members in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-002.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 12 | Management made the necessary adjustment.


Collections from sale of bid documents are now recorded as Trust Liabilities and in accordance with the provisions of COA- Circular No. 2013-002

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure that succeeding payment of BAC honorarium sourced out from the agency savings shall be supported with a Sangguniang Bayan ordinance in accordance with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 and R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 13 | BAC Honoraria under DV No. 100-2109-0762 was disbursed from the collection of bid documents recorded as trust liabilities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to stop the grant of cash advances to ineligible employees and ensure that henceforth, only regular and special cash advances shall be granted to its officials and employees in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular  No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 14 | Cash advances granted under DV Nos. 100-2103-0149 and 100-2106-0408 were in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees unless their previous cash advance has been settled/ liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts.

We  have  recommended further  that the Municipal Accountant attach a certification in the disbursement vouchers of claimants that previous cash advance has been settled/ liquidated, and accounted for in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 15 | For CY 2021, Management complied with the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.


Also, certifications from the Municipal Accountant that no outstanding unliquidated cash advance of the employees concern were attached to DV Nos. 100-2103-0149 and 100-2106-0408.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management submit valid and justifiable reasons for the grant of 50 percent or more of accumulated leave credits to its two employees for the audit team’s validation.

Henceforth, Management agreed to refrain from granting monetization of accumulated leave credits to its employees which is not in accordance with the provisions of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 16 | Management submitted the required documents. 



Management granted monetization of leave credits under DV No. 100-2105-0340 in accordance with the provisions of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular 2012-001.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management fast-track the implementation of the project in order to serve its purpose as a quarantine facility for PUIs during this time of pandemic.

We have recommended further r and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure that contract time extensions granted to contractors are valid and justifiable in accordance with the provisions of Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 17 | The project was already completed and utilized for quarantine facility.


Management ensured that contract time extensions granted to contractors are valid and justifiable in accordance with Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to advise the Municipal Engineer to properly supervise the implementation of infrastructure projects to avoid delay and to attain the desired objectives of the projects.


Henceforth, Management agreed to ensure that all infrastructure projects be satisfactorily completed within the specified target completion dates in order for its constituents to enjoy the timely utilization of the project and its intended benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
Part II of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 18 | For CY 2021, the Office of the Municipal Engineer properly supervised the implementation of infrastructure projects to be satisfactorily completed within the specified target completion dates.


Management ensured that all infrastructure projects were satisfactorily completed within the target completion dates.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to spearhead the establishment of the One Town One Product Store Express at Kayan East, Tadian to make it operational and achieve the objective of the DTI-funded Bottoms-Up-Budgeting Project.
observations.managementAction:
Part III of CY 2020 AAR, Observation No. 10 | A publication for interested organizations/ individuals to operate the One Town One Product Store Express was posted. 
Accordingly, an organization signified interest in the operation of the store, thus, Management has prepared the lease agreement for signing.
observations.reason: To date, the interested organization has yet to enter into a lease agreement with Management, thus, the store is still inoperative.
